title: Arnold--Nielsen Geometry for Complexity-Deformed Noncommutative Transport
abstract: We deform the Carlen--Maas--Wirth framework for noncommutative dynamical optimal transport by an Arnold--Nielsen type complexity operator. A positive state-independent operator $G$ compatible with the Hilbert bimodule structure of a noncommutative differential calculus $\partial\colon\M\to\Hcal$ can be absorbed into the calculus itself, \[ \partial_G:=G^{1/2}\partial. \] The corresponding complexity-weighted transport problem is exactly the unweighted transport problem generated by $\partial_G$, whenever the deformed quadratic form remains Dirichlet. In finite dimensions we prove existence of minimizers for density-dependent Petz-class metrics and for fixed physical complexity weights, the latter without commutation between $G$ and the state-dependent mobility. On unitary orbits we identify the induced distance with a quotient metric coming from a right-invariant complexity geometry. This yields an exact Bell-state preparation result via Clairaut's relation and an exactly computed restricted-path upper bound for GHZ preparation; the Lindblad detailed-balance case is included only as entropy-gradient-flow background.

bite.hook: Deforming quantum transport geometry with complexity operators enables exact paths for preparing Bell states.
tldr: Deformed the Carlen–Maas–Wirth quantum transport framework using an Arnold–Nielsen complexity operator.
Absorbed complexity operators into differential calculus to evaluate state-dependent mobility quotient metrics.
Derived exact geometric paths for Bell state preparation and restricted-path upper bounds for GHZ states.
bite.whyItMatters: Connecting quantum state preparation limits directly to geometric optimal transport provides exact mathematical bounds for constructing complex entanglement.
bite.eli5: Think of finding the quickest route on a map where terrain difficulty varies; geometry tools help plot the absolute best shortcut to craft fragile quantum states.

glossary.shortDef: The mathematical study of efficiently moving mass between probability distributions.
glossary.eli5Def: Finding the cheapest way to move a pile of dirt from one place to reshape it into another.

glossary.shortDef: One of four maximally entangled quantum states of two qubits.
glossary.eli5Def: A pair of quantum particles connected so intimately that measuring one instantly tells you about the other.

glossary.shortDef: An entangled quantum state involving three or more subsystems.
glossary.eli5Def: A team of three or more quantum bits that are all linked together simultaneously.
